Alcohol use disorder is a chronic, relapsing condition that requires lifelong management but ongoing continuing care is almost non-existent.  

We are developing a digital therapeutic app to address this gap.

To scale this app, we use large language models to communicate with patients and the language used may affect engagement.

I developed a framework to use LLMs to generate messages within the app and had participants with AUD rate message helpfulness.  

Our analyses concluded that we could maximize message helpfulness by selecting specific tones and styles for each individual using a machine learning model.
